title: dalea albiflora
intro: Dalea albiflora, also known as the white prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Dalea albiflora can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being.
